notes: AutoCAD *D#### dimension block: MTEXT '325' equals bbox span 325; DIM-layer extension+dimension lines (8E44/8E45/8E46), DIMDOT terminators, DEFPOINTS defpoints -- cached dimension, contains no wall lines.

rationale.evidence: The block name '*D375' follows AutoCAD's *D#### convention for anonymous dimension blocks, and its 9 entities confirm it: on layer DIM sit two vertical extension LINEs (8E44/8E45), one horizontal dimension LINE (8E46), two DIMDOT INSERT terminators (8E47/8E48) and one MTEXT (8E49) reading '325'; three POINTs (8E4A/8E4B/8E4C) sit on DEFPOINTS as the associative definition points. The MTEXT value 325 equals the bbox horizontal span (55403.83->55728.83 = 325) to the millimetre, so the text is a measured dimension, not a free label.
rationale.rule: A block named *D#### whose lines are on a DIM layer carrying DIMDOT/arrow terminators plus a DEFPOINTS definition-point trio, and whose text equals its own geometric span, is a cached (exploded/anonymous) dimension in any AutoCAD drawing -- annotation, not building fabric. Extension and dimension lines are annotation geometry and never count as walls.
rationale.counterfactual: Had the three LINEs been on a wall/structural layer as an offset parallel pair forming a thickness, with no DIMDOT terminators, no span-matching MTEXT and no DEFPOINTS points, the block would classify as plan/wall geometry (평면 부분도) instead. Removing only the text-equals-span match would leave it a leader or generic symbol rather than a dimension.
rationale.uncertainty: This projection shows the definition in isolation, not its INSERT sites, so I cannot tell what feature each dimension measures (wall run, opening, or furniture edge) or how often it is placed. Linetype, colour and any XDATA are omitted, so I cannot fully distinguish a live associative dimension from a frozen graphical copy.

rationale.evidence: The block name '*D391' follows AutoCAD's *D#### convention for anonymous dimension blocks, and its 9 entities confirm it: on layer DIM sit two vertical extension LINEs (8ED4/8ED5), one horizontal dimension LINE (8ED6), two DIMDOT INSERT terminators (8ED7/8ED8) and one MTEXT (8ED9) reading '1820'; three POINTs (8EDA/8EDB/8EDC) sit on DEFPOINTS as the associative definition points. The MTEXT value 1820 equals the bbox horizontal span (52328.83->54148.83 = 1820) to the millimetre, so the text is a measured dimension, not a free label.
rationale.rule: A block named *D#### whose lines are on a DIM layer carrying DIMDOT/arrow terminators plus a DEFPOINTS definition-point trio, and whose text equals its own geometric span, is a cached (exploded/anonymous) dimension in any AutoCAD drawing -- annotation, not building fabric. Extension and dimension lines are annotation geometry and never count as walls.
rationale.counterfactual: Had the three LINEs been on a wall/structural layer as an offset parallel pair forming a thickness, with no DIMDOT terminators, no span-matching MTEXT and no DEFPOINTS points, the block would classify as plan/wall geometry (평면 부분도) instead. Removing only the text-equals-span match would leave it a leader or generic symbol rather than a dimension.
rationale.uncertainty: This projection shows the definition in isolation, not its INSERT sites, so I cannot tell what feature each dimension measures (wall run, opening, or furniture edge) or how often it is placed. Linetype, colour and any XDATA are omitted, so I cannot fully distinguish a live associative dimension from a frozen graphical copy.
